flowTitle: XIIGen Social Platform - Friend Requests and Social Feed

freedomConfigAdaptation.description: Tenant-safe values that can change at fork time without changing friend-request state, privacy checks, tenant scope, or event ordering.

keys.semantic: Maximum number of feed items generated for one accepted connection activity.

boundedRange.rationale: Keeps feed fan-out bounded while allowing denser or quieter tenant communities.

keys.semantic: Controls how the feed scorer balances recency, relationship strength, and activity type.

boundedRange.rationale: Weights are AI-scoring inputs; score=0 remains valid and delivery filtering stays in T78.

keys.semantic: Minimum score required before a feed item is delivered to a recipient.

boundedRange.rationale: Score filtering is tenant-tunable, but below-threshold remains a successful suppressed delivery decision.

keys.semantic: Upper bound on feed insert fan-out for one connection activity.

boundedRange.rationale: Protects tenant feed volume while preserving delivery ordering and privacy re-checks.

keys.semantic: Allowed delivery channels for social notifications after recipient consent succeeds.

grammarAdaptation.description: FLOW-07 has no tenant-editable DSL grammar surface.

roleScopeAdaptation.description: FLOW-07 role branching is implemented through role-aware client surfaces and server authorization. Tenants may tune copy and feed behavior, not bypass role isolation.

businessDomainAdaptation.description: Domain policies a tenant can tune through existing FREEDOM keys while MACHINE invariants remain fixed.

edgeCases.name: Quiet community feed
edgeCases.semantic: A tenant can raise the delivery threshold and reduce feed fan-out for smaller trusted communities.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T78 treats below-threshold delivery as a successful non-delivery result.

edgeCases.name: Freshness-weighted feed
edgeCases.semantic: A tenant can favor recent relationship activity without changing AI scoring or privacy checks.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T77 passes weights into the scoring provider; score=0 remains valid.

edgeCases.name: Notification channel policy
edgeCases.semantic: A tenant can allow push-only, email-only, or in-app notification channels after consent passes.
edgeCases.currentImplementation: T79 checks consent before channel selection and stores notification records privately.

ironRulesMACHINE.description: Fixed invariants that tenants cannot change without breaking portability or authorization certification.
rules: Friend requests stay tenant-private and cannot write cross-tenant graph edges.
T76 calls PrivacyGatekeeperService before storing or emitting generated feed items.
T78 re-checks privacy unconditionally before delivery, even when T76 already checked.
Score 0 is valid; delivery filtering happens only in T78.
Notification consent is checked before channel selection and dispatch.
storeDocument completes before enqueue for feed, delivery, notification, graph, and analytics events.
All services remain FLOW_SCOPED and return DataProcessResult.

tenant-a-quiet-feed.description: Tenant A runs a smaller professional community and wants fewer low-signal feed deliveries.
tenant-a-quiet-feed.impact: The feed becomes more selective while privacy re-checks, graph writes, event names, and authorization stay unchanged.
tenant-a-quiet-feed.testable: Tenant A adapted visual evidence shows quieter feed positioning/copy, and service tests still prove below-threshold delivery is a success result.

tenant-a-fresh-feed.description: Tenant A wants recent activity to outrank older popularity signals.
tenant-a-fresh-feed.impact: Scoring becomes more time-sensitive while delivery and privacy invariants remain fixed.
tenant-a-fresh-feed.testable: FeedScorerService receives the adapted FREEDOM weight map and still emits scored items when score is 0.

notPartOfAdaptationSurface.description: MACHINE elements that must not change during tenant adaptation.
elements: Task type IDs T73 through T82

passCriteria: Tenant A can apply a TYPE-A value change without altering MACHINE constraints.
The adapted value is present in tenant.config.json adaptationHistory.
The adapted value is visible in Tenant A visual evidence.
Auth checks still return 401 for missing token, 401 for cross-tenant token, and 403 for wrong-role token after adaptation.
